title: GM Oracle: Yes or No
description: Degrees of yes and no for improv oracle answers — from hard refusal to emphatic success, each with narrative flavor and implied complication.

entries.result: No. The door is shut, the window is barred, and anyone who asks again will be remembered.
No — and the refusal comes with an edge that makes asking again unwise.
No. Whatever was possible here has passed.
No. The answer was always no; the asker simply hadn't known it yet.
No — and the situation is worse for the asking.
No. The opportunity has closed, and something new has moved into its place.
No. Forces beyond anyone's immediate control have already decided this.
No, and word of the attempt gets back to someone who matters.
No — firmly, clearly, and with no room left for negotiation.
No. The ground here is less stable than it appeared.
No. Something is in the way that will not move.
No — but the refusal reveals more about the situation than a yes ever would have.
No. There is no path through this direction. Another must be found.
No, and the refusal closes an adjacent option as well.
No. Whatever was sought here is either gone, wrong, or was never real.
No — and the denial comes with a warning.
No. The timing is wrong, and that window is now shut.
No — not now, not like this, and possibly not ever.
No. The one who could have helped has already decided otherwise.
No. This course leads somewhere worse.
No — and the reason is something the party didn't know to account for.
No. The answer was predetermined before anyone arrived.
No, and an obstacle that seemed minor reveals itself as the whole problem.
No. A hidden factor has been working against this outcome from the start.
No — the refusal is final, and pressing it will cost something.
No — but there is another route, if the party is willing to take the longer road.
No — but a partial version of what was sought may still be available.
No — but someone nearby knows who can actually help.
No — but the refusal comes with useful information attached.
No — but the situation isn't as closed as it appears. Something remains.
No — but a small concession comes with the refusal, almost as an apology.
No — but if circumstances were slightly different, the answer would change.
No — but the opposition isn't total. There's a crack in the wall.
No — but a third party might be willing to act where this one won't.
No — but the timing is the problem, not the request itself.
No — but something left behind in the aftermath is worth picking up.
No — but a door closes here and another creaks open nearby.
No — but whoever is responsible will tell someone else. It may reach the right ears.
No — but the refusal happens to reveal exactly what the party needed to know.
No — but the person saying no is not entirely convinced.
No — but a single condition, if met, could flip the answer.
No — but the party leaves with more goodwill than they arrived with.
No — but someone in the background quietly disagrees with the refusal.
No — but the problem identified here points directly at the actual solution.
No — but a favor owed to someone else might be leveraged here.
No — but the refusal is honest, which is more than most answers offer.
No — but something useful falls out of the failed attempt anyway.
No — but there's a back way in, if anyone bothers to look for it.
No — but the party's reputation here improves slightly despite the setback.
No — but an unexpected witness to the attempt turns out to be sympathetic.
No — but the denial came too quickly, which says something.
No — but the refusal is tactical, not genuine. It can be worked around.
No — but they'd be willing to be persuaded if approached differently.
No — but a complication the party didn't cause may clear the path later.
No — but the other side of this failure is a clean slate.
No — but an alternative emerges that the party hadn't considered.
No — but the damage is contained. Nothing has been made worse.
No — but the failure is quiet. No one who matters has noticed.
No — but the situation has shifted in a way that may eventually help.
No — but a small piece of what was wanted arrives anyway, unbidden.
No — but the refuser is embarrassed about it, which is leverage.
No — but the timing suggests trying again soon is worth considering.
No — but there's a workaround that is, technically speaking, legal.
No — but someone who is owed a debt steps in at the margins.
No — but an old connection resurfaces just in time to soften the blow.
The situation is unclear. More information is needed before anything resolves.
Yes and no. Both are true at once, which is more complicated than either alone.
It's possible — but the conditions are narrow and may not hold.
Unclear. The answer depends on a factor that hasn't revealed itself yet.
Maybe — but the odds aren't favorable, and something will have to give.
The situation is in flux. Whatever is true now may not be true by morning.
Uncertain. A third party holds the deciding factor and hasn't moved yet.
It could go either way. The next action taken will tip the balance.
Something is blocking a clean answer. The picture is obscured.
Possibly — but the path to yes runs through a complication first.
Unknown. The right person to ask isn't here, and the wrong person will guess.
The answer is close to yes but not there yet. Something small stands in the way.
Ambiguous — the outcome depends on a variable the party hasn't encountered.
It's contested. Two forces are pulling the situation in opposite directions.
The situation resists a clean answer. Both outcomes are live.
Not yet decided. Something is still in motion.
The truthful answer is: we'll see. No one here knows for certain.
Yes, if everything goes right. And not everything has gone right.
On the edge. The smallest push could resolve this in either direction.
Something is standing between the party and a clear answer. It may be important.
Yes — but it takes longer than expected.
Yes — but the cost is higher than anticipated.
Yes — but something is slightly wrong with what was obtained.
Yes — but a complication tags along that wasn't part of the plan.
Yes — but someone notices, and that will matter later.
Yes — but only partially. What's available is less than what was needed.
Yes — but the method required leaves a mark.
Yes — but it comes with strings that weren't mentioned up front.
Yes — but the thing gained is already in use by someone else.
Yes — but the answer costs something intangible: goodwill, reputation, or trust.
Yes — but it only works once. A second attempt won't succeed the same way.
Yes — but it requires help, and help requires a favor owed.
Yes — but a bystander is inconvenienced, and they remember faces.
Yes — but the situation changes during the attempt.
Yes — but someone has to be left behind, at least for now.
Yes — but it draws attention the party didn't want.
Yes — but what's found isn't exactly what was sought.
Yes — but a clock has started that wasn't running before.
Yes — but the outcome produces a new problem alongside the solved one.
Yes — but the success is partial in a way that leaves the task unfinished.
Yes — but something that was safe before the attempt is now at risk.
Yes — but the route through creates an obligation.
Yes — but the timing is awkward. Something else was disturbed.
Yes — but a piece of the outcome is already in someone else's hands.
Yes — but the success has a witness who isn't friendly.
Yes — but it comes through an unexpected intermediary.
Yes — but a tool or resource is spent in the process.
Yes — but the party's position is now exposed in a way it wasn't before.
Yes — but the win is smaller than it looked from the outside.
Yes — but one member of the group is worse off than before.
Yes — but the attempt has started a rumor.
Yes — but the result is fragile. It won't survive being tested too hard.
Yes — but there's a timer now. Act before the situation resets.
Yes — but something was overlooked during the attempt.
Yes — but it creates a dependency that didn't exist before.
Yes — but the resolution is messy. It works, but not cleanly.
Yes — but the opposition now knows more about the party than before.
Yes — but a minor promise was implied in the process and will be remembered.
Yes — but whatever succeeded here can't be repeated the same way.
Yes — but the victory is visible in a way that complicates the next move.
Yes — but it took more effort than it should have, and that's information.
Yes — but a door that was open before the attempt is now closed.
Yes — but the person who enabled the success wants something in return.
Yes — but the advantage gained is temporary. Enjoy it while it holds.
Yes — but someone is going to ask questions that will be hard to answer.
Yes — but a resource that seemed unlimited has just hit its limit.
Yes — but there's a side effect that wasn't anticipated.
Yes — but the success reveals an even larger problem underneath.
Yes — but a previously unknown complication is now known.
Yes — but another party got there first and left something behind.
Yes — but the approach required cutting a corner that may come up later.
Yes — but success here telegraphs intention to someone watching.
Yes — but the thing gained is already degrading. Time is short.
Yes — but the method worked this time and may not work again.
Yes — but something was disturbed in the process that will need tending.
Yes — but it comes at the cost of something the party didn't know it was spending.
Yes — but the help required to make it happen comes with an agenda.
Yes — but the action cannot be undone, and undoing it may become necessary.
Yes — but a loose end was created and hasn't been tied off.
Yes — but the path forward is now narrower than it was before the attempt.
Yes — but the problem solved here has been replaced by a slightly different one.
Yes — but a small lie was required, and small lies have long memories.
Yes — but the success changes the dynamics of the group in a subtle way.
Yes — but whoever is watching has updated their assessment of the party.
Yes — but something that helped this time will not be available next time.
Yes — and it happens faster than anyone expected.
Yes — and a bonus arrives alongside what was sought.
Yes — and the timing couldn't be better.
Yes — and an unexpected ally appears in the process.
Yes — and the way it unfolds reveals something useful.
Yes — and the aftermath is cleaner than anticipated.
Yes — and a second opportunity opens up while the first is being taken.
Yes — and someone important notices and is impressed.
Yes — and more is available than was asked for.
Yes — and an obstacle elsewhere is incidentally resolved.
Yes — and the result is better than the party had any reason to expect.
Yes — and a useful piece of information comes along with the success.
Yes — and the opposition is set back further than the immediate action suggested.
Yes — and a connection is made that will be worth something later.
Yes — and the party's reputation in this area improves.
Yes — and whatever was limiting this situation is now gone.
Yes — and the path forward is clearer than it has been in some time.
Yes — and a resource previously thought spent turns out to have more in it.
Yes — and the success triggers something helpful that was lying dormant.
Yes — and the person who helped is now invested in the outcome.
Yes — and something the party had lost track of turns up in the process.
Yes — and the momentum created here will carry into the next challenge.
Yes — and the victory is witnessed by someone who will spread word of it.
Yes — and the effort required was less than projected.
Yes — and a long-standing complication is resolved as a side effect.
Yes — and the party exits the situation in a stronger position than it entered.
Yes — and an adversary's plan takes a meaningful hit.
Yes — and the groundwork is laid for something even larger.
Yes — and the success produces a gift the party didn't know it needed.
Yes — and a door that was previously locked swings open.
Yes, unambiguously and completely.
Yes — exactly as hoped, without complication.
Yes. It works, it holds, and nothing is made worse.
Yes. The answer was always available; everything just aligned.
Yes, immediately and without resistance.
Yes. The outcome is exactly what the situation required.
Yes, and the margin of success is wide enough to feel safe.
Yes. What was needed was here all along.
Yes — no caveats, no costs, no fine print.
Yes. The circumstances couldn't be more favorable.
Yes, and the result is durable. This one will last.
Yes. The door opens, the path is clear, and the timing is right.
Yes — better than expected and more complete than was asked for.
Yes. The opposition folds without incident.
Yes, cleanly and without leaving a trail.
Yes. The plan works because the plan was good.
Yes — and the ease of it should be enjoyed, because it won't always be this simple.
Yes. Everything that needed to go right did.
Yes, and the success feels earned. It was.
Yes. Full stop.
